creator: grahamperrin@gmail.com
text:
A few weeks ago at <https://man.freebsd.org/cgi/man.cgi?query=glob&apropos=1> I noticed that for some ports, manual pages do not have suitably formed NAME sections. 

Bug 273248 intends to fix this for kitty.conf(5). 

From the apropos results above, I have a shortlist of other ports that might be improved e.g. upstream. 

I experimented with a different search. <https://man.freebsd.org/cgi/man.cgi?query=sort&apropos=1> reveals more misfits, such as certbot(1). 

----

To work around affected ports that will not (or can not) be enhanced, is it possible for man.freebsd.org representations to be somehow stemmed? 

Stemming after the first two _perceptible_ paragraphs might be enough. Food for thought: 

<https://man.freebsd.org/cgi/man.cgi?query=samba-dcerpcd&sektion=8&manpath=freebsd-ports>

<https://man.freebsd.org/cgi/man.cgi?query=virtualenv&sektion=1&manpath=freebsd-ports>

Thanks
